name: Athens
localTips: Validate your metro or bus ticket at the orange machines before boarding — random inspections happen and fines are steep even for a genuine mistake.
Restaurants directly on Plaka's main tourist strips tend to overcharge — walk a block or two into Psiri or Anafiotika for the same food at local prices.
Many archaeological sites, including the Acropolis, offer free entry on the first Sunday of the month from November to March — check current dates before you go.
Summer afternoons regularly hit 35°C+ — visit the Acropolis and other exposed sites at opening time or after 5pm, and always carry water.
A single combined ticket covers the Acropolis and six other major sites for five days — buy it online in advance to skip the entrance queue entirely.

activities.title: Areopagus Hill Acropolis view
activities.description: A worn marble outcrop below the Acropolis where St. Paul once preached — climb the polished rock (slippery when wet) for a free, close-up view of the Parthenon at sunset.

activities.title: Plaka old town walk
activities.description: Neoclassical houses, bougainvillea-draped lanes and tiny squares beneath the Acropolis — Athens' oldest continuously inhabited neighbourhood, pedestrian and easy to get lost in.

activities.title: Anafiotika whitewashed lanes
activities.description: A tiny Cycladic-style village tucked into the Acropolis's northern slope, built by island stonemasons in the 1800s — narrow whitewashed alleys that feel like Santorini in central Athens.

activities.title: National Garden shaded paths
activities.description: A 19th-century royal garden behind the Parliament, with duck ponds, a small zoo and Athens' best shade — a genuine escape from the heat right in the city centre.

activities.title: Changing of the Evzones guard
activities.description: Ceremonial guards in traditional kilts and pom-pom clogs perform a slow-motion changeover at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier every hour — the Sunday 11am version is the full parade.

activities.title: Monastiraki flea market
activities.description: A sprawling maze of antique dealers, record shops and souvenir stalls beneath the Acropolis, busiest and best on Sunday mornings when it spills into every side street.

activities.title: Temple of Olympian Zeus view
activities.description: Fifteen towering Corinthian columns, all that remains of what was once Greece's largest temple — visible in full from the free public street and park around the fenced site.

activities.title: Panathenaic Stadium exterior
activities.description: The gleaming all-marble stadium that hosted the first modern Olympics in 1896 — its horseshoe form and gates are striking to see from the surrounding streets at no cost.

activities.title: Lycabettus Hill sunset climb
activities.description: Athens' highest point, crowned by a tiny whitewashed chapel — the switchback path up is free (only the funicular charges), and the 360° sunset view takes in the Acropolis and the sea.

activities.title: Varvakios Central Market
activities.description: A raucous covered meat, fish and produce market that's been running since 1886 — vendors shouting prices, whole swordfish on ice, and stalls selling every Greek spice imaginable.

activities.title: Psiri street art wander
activities.description: A once-gritty industrial quarter now covered in some of Athens' best large-scale murals, with graffiti tours self-guided just by wandering the back alleys.

activities.title: Filopappou Hill sunset walk
activities.description: A pine-covered hill opposite the Acropolis with quieter paths, ancient carved rock-cut seats and one of the classic postcard views of the Parthenon across the valley.

activities.title: Exarcheia street art & anarchist quarter
activities.description: Athens' most politically charged neighbourhood, its walls layered with murals, slogans and protest art — a raw contrast to Plaka's postcard prettiness a few streets away.

activities.title: Christmas lights at Syntagma Square
activities.description: A giant lit tree and festive decorations fill Syntagma through December, with the National Garden strung with lights along its edges.

activities.title: Coffee in the Numismatic Museum garden
activities.description: The neoclassical former mansion of Heinrich Schliemann hides a quiet marble-courtyard café garden behind it — one of central Athens' best-kept shaded secrets.

activities.title: Café culture in Kolonaki
activities.description: Athens' most upscale neighbourhood, where locals linger for hours at pavement cafés on Plateia Kolonaki — better people-watching than Plaka, with none of the tourist crowds.
activities.insiderNote: Order a freddo espresso, the Greek summer classic — locals drink it iced year-round, not just when it's hot.

activities.title: Late-night patsas soup at the market
activities.description: After the fish stalls close, a handful of tiny patsatzidika taverns inside and around the Central Market serve tripe soup to butchers, taxi drivers and clubbers until dawn.
activities.insiderNote: It's an acquired taste but a genuine Athens ritual — go around 2–4am on a weekend to see the market's true nocturnal life.

activities.title: Rooftop bar with Acropolis view
activities.description: Several unmarked hotel rooftop bars around Monastiraki and Plaka give a floodlit Acropolis view without the crowds of the famous named ones — ask locals for that week's favourite.
activities.insiderNote: Rooftops rotate in popularity — check A for Athens or 360 Cocktail Bar first, then ask the bartender where locals are actually going this month.

activities.title: Backstreet taverna in Psiri
activities.description: Small family-run tavernas in Psiri's side streets serve mezedes and grilled meat at prices half of Plaka's, with plastic chairs spilling onto the cobblestones after dark.
activities.insiderNote: Order a mixed meze plate for the table rather than individual mains — it's the local way to try more dishes for less.

activities.title: Live rembetiko music tavern
activities.description: Rembetiko — Greece's blues, born in 1920s refugee neighbourhoods — is still played live in small smoky tavernas, with bouzouki players and locals dancing between tables.
activities.insiderNote: Venues shift often; ask at a local kafeneio in Exarcheia or check listings for that week's rembetiko night rather than a fixed 'tourist' venue.

activities.title: Free Thursday evening at the Benaki Museum
activities.description: One of Athens' best private collections of Greek art and history, spanning antiquity to the 20th century, offers free entry on Thursday evenings.
activities.insiderNote: The rooftop café stays open with the free hours and has one of the best sunset views over the National Garden — arrive with time to spare for a drink.

activities.title: Zappeion Gardens hidden paths
activities.description: The formal gardens around the Zappeion exhibition hall are a favourite jogging and dog-walking spot for locals, quieter than the adjoining National Garden most of the day.
activities.insiderNote: The small café inside the Zappeion building itself is a peaceful, air-conditioned break most tourists walk straight past.

activities.title: Loukoumades at a proper kiosk
activities.description: Warm honey-soaked doughnut balls are Athens' favourite street dessert — Krinos near the Central Market has been frying them the same way since 1922.
activities.insiderNote: Order them with cinnamon and crushed walnuts rather than plain honey — the traditional local topping combination.

activities.title: Acropolis & Parthenon entry
activities.description: Walk up through the Propylaea gate to the Parthenon and Erechtheion — the single essential Athens experience, best done at opening to beat both crowds and heat.
activities.bookingQuery: Acropolis and Parthenon skip the line ticket Athens

activities.title: Acropolis Museum entry
activities.description: A glass-floored modern museum built over active excavations, displaying original Parthenon sculptures with a top-floor gallery aligned exactly with the Acropolis outside.

activities.title: National Archaeological Museum entry
activities.description: Greece's largest museum — the golden Mask of Agamemnon, the Antikythera Mechanism and room after room of Cycladic, Mycenaean and classical treasures.
activities.bookingQuery: National Archaeological Museum entry ticket Athens

activities.title: Kerameikos ancient cemetery entry
activities.description: The ancient city's main cemetery and pottery quarter, with marble grave stelae still standing among cypress trees — one of Athens' quietest and least crowded archaeological sites.
activities.bookingQuery: Kerameikos ancient cemetery entry ticket Athens

activities.title: Cape Sounio Temple of Poseidon sunset tour
activities.description: A coastal drive to the dramatic clifftop Temple of Poseidon, timed to watch the sun set behind its columns over the Aegean — one of Greece's most photographed sunsets.
activities.bookingQuery: Cape Sounio Temple of Poseidon sunset tour from Athens

activities.title: Delphi day trip
activities.description: The ancient sanctuary of the Oracle, dramatically set on Mount Parnassus's slopes — a full-day trip that includes the temple ruins, theatre and stadium plus the on-site museum.

activities.title: Greek night: folk dance & dinner show
activities.description: A lively evening of live bouzouki music, traditional dancing and plate-smashing (in designated areas) paired with a multi-course Greek dinner.
activities.bookingQuery: Greek night folk dance dinner show Athens

activities.title: Food tour through Psiri & Plaka
activities.description: Taste souvlaki, loukoumades, Greek cheeses and local wine across several stops with a guide who explains the history behind each dish along the way.

activities.title: Aegina, Poros & Hydra one-day cruise
activities.description: A boat trip to three Saronic Gulf islands in a single day, each with a completely different character, from pistachio groves to car-free cobbled Hydra.
activities.bookingQuery: Aegina Poros Hydra one day cruise from Athens

activities.title: Athens Riviera sunset catamaran cruise
activities.description: A short evening sail along the Athens Riviera coastline with drinks on board, watching the sun drop into the Saronic Gulf from the water.
